system_message: You are an expert in the Serverless Workflow Specification. Your role is to analyze and understand workflow rules and descriptions from the specification, rephrase them for clarity, and explain their components with precision. You focus on teaching and maintaining compliance with the specification’s standards.

questions: Rephrase the`{title}` to make it clearer and easier to understand, incorporating all details from the full description.
Identify the key concepts in the `{title}` and explain their significance within the context of the Serverless Workflow Specification, using all available information.
Highlight the main rules or principles described for `{title}` and explain their importance in workflow design, referencing the full description.
Break down the `{title}` into smaller, more digestible components, and elaborate on each part to improve comprehension while covering the entire description.
Discuss how the `{title}` aligns with the goals of the Serverless Workflow Specification, using all details from the description.
Identify ambiguities or areas in the `{title}` that could be clarified or expanded for better understanding, ensuring the full description is considered.
Suggest ways to enhance the `{title}` to emphasize its alignment with the specification rules, incorporating the full description.
Analyze the `{title}` and provide a concise summary that highlights its key points and relevance, while ensuring the full description is captured.

Description and Code Relationship.condition: {{ 'code' in element and element.code and element.code == true }}
questions: Explain how the `{title}` relates to the provided code, focusing on the alignment between the two and using all details from the full description.
Identify components in the code of `{title}` that reflect the description and explain their role, ensuring the explanation uses the full description.
Discuss how the code of `{title}` implements the rules or principles described in the specification, referencing the entire description.
Provide a detailed explanation of how the description of `{title}` and code together define a compliant workflow, ensuring the full description is included.

Code Understanding and Generation.condition: {{ 'code' in element and element.code and element.code == true }}
questions: Display the full code of `{title}` and explain how it adheres to the rules in the description, referencing all details from the description.
Provide a step-by-step explanation of the code for `{title}`, linking each part to its purpose in the description and covering all the information in the full description.
Generate a JSON/YAML representation of `{title}` based on the description and the provided code, ensuring the output reflects the entire description.
Discuss how the code of `{title}` enables workflow execution and aligns with the description’s principles, covering all aspects of the description.
Explain how each element in the code contributes to achieving the goals described for `{title}`, referencing all details from the full description.

Specification Rule Application to Code.condition: {{ 'code' in element and element.code and element.code == true }}
questions: Describe how the rules in the Serverless Workflow Specification are reflected in the code for `{title}`, using all details from the full description.
Identify potential improvements to the code of `{title}` to better align with the description or specification rules, ensuring all aspects of the description are considered.
Provide examples of how the code for `{title}` can be reused or extended in other workflows, referencing the full description.
Suggest additional features or adjustments to the code of `{title}` to improve modularity and compliance, incorporating all details from the description.
Summarize the description of the workflow `{name}` by highlighting its primary purpose and functionality.
Analyze the description of the workflow `{name}` to identify its key components and explain their roles in enabling the workflow's functionality.
Describe the specific problem the workflow `{name}` addresses and how it contributes to the overall system or application.
Decompose the workflow `{name}` into its core elements, including states, transitions, functions, and actions, and provide a detailed explanation of the role each component plays.
Analyze the workflow `{name}` by outlining its key structural components and explaining how they contribute to its seamless execution and reliability.
Explain the relationships between the states and transitions in the workflow `{name}`, highlighting how data flows and decisions are managed.
Provide a detailed walkthrough of how the workflow `{name}` processes inputs, manages transitions, and produces outputs through its states and functions.
Provide an example scenario where the workflow `{name}` could be used effectively.
Discuss how the workflow `{name}` can be modified or extended to support additional use cases.
Explain how this workflow `{name}` contributes to modularity and scalability in workflow design.
Display the full code of the workflow `{name}` and include it verbatim in the output, followed by a detailed explanation of each section and its contribution to the workflow’s overall functionality.

Present the complete workflow `{name}` code in its entirety, then describe the purpose of each major component, such as states, transitions, actions, and functions, ensuring the code is fully visible in the output.
Provide the full YAML or JSON code of the workflow `{name}` exactly as defined, and offer a section-by-section walkthrough to explain its adherence to the Serverless Workflow Specification.
Include the full code of the workflow `{name}` in the output, highlighting critical elements like annotations, metadata, and configuration settings, and explain their significance.

Display the entire workflow `{name}` code and explain how it manages error handling, retries, and data processing, ensuring the code is visible and the explanation links directly to the relevant sections.
Extract a code snippet from the workflow `{name}` that defines a critical state and explain its functionality.
Provide a snippet of the workflow `{name}` showing data input handling and explain how it works.
Generate a snippet showing error handling or retry logic in the workflow `{name}` and explain its purpose.
Explain how the properties defined in the workflow `{name}` are utilized in its code to configure execution and behavior.
Identify specific sections of the workflow `{name}` code that demonstrate the use of properties, and explain how they influence workflow operations.
Discuss how the properties in the workflow `{name}` define key aspects such as metadata, data inputs, and execution constraints, and how these are reflected in the code.
Analyze the role of properties in the workflow `{name}` and explain how they enable customization, portability, and alignment with the workflow’s purpose.
Provide examples from the workflow `{name}` showing how changes to properties, such as timeouts or annotations, directly affect its behavior or functionality.
properties_to_code.condition: {{ 'properties' in element and element.properties and element.properties != '' }}

questions: Describe how metadata in the workflow `{name}` (e.g., annotations, descriptions) aligns with the workflow’s goals, and how the properties file contributes to this alignment.
Explain the relationship between the execution context defined in the properties file and its implementation in the workflow code.
Discuss how the properties file ensures the workflow `{name}` remains portable and adaptable across different environments.
metadata_and_execution_context.condition: {{ 'properties' in element and element.properties and element.properties != '' }}

questions: Explain how properties defined in the properties file are referenced using `$SECRET` in the workflow `{name}` to dynamically configure execution and behavior.
Identify specific sections of the workflow `{name}` code where `$SECRET` is used to access properties defined in the properties file, and explain their role in influencing workflow operations.
Discuss how the workflow `{name}` uses `$SECRET` to dynamically integrate property values from the properties file, ensuring flexibility and adaptability during execution.

Analyze the relationship between the properties defined in the properties file and their usage through `$SECRET` in the workflow `{name}`, explaining how this interaction supports runtime configurations and aligns with workflow goals.

Provide examples from the workflow `{name}` showing how specific properties defined in the properties file are accessed via `$SECRET`, and explain how changes to these properties directly impact workflow behavior and functionality.
properties_secret_relation_to_workflow.condition: {{ 'properties' in element and element.properties and element.properties != '' }}

questions: If the input schema exists, describe how it validates and enforces the structure of incoming data in the workflow `{name}`; if the output schema exists, explain how it defines the format and correctness of data produced after execution.

If the input schema exists, discuss how it ensures the integrity and correctness of data required for execution; if the output schema exists, explain how it ensures compatibility with downstream systems or services consuming the results.

If the input schema exists, provide examples showing how it defines constraints, types, or formats for incoming data in the workflow `{name}`; if the output schema exists, provide examples of how it handles constraints and required fields for outgoing data.

If the input schema exists, explain how it interacts with states and actions to process data effectively in the workflow `{name}`; if the output schema exists, describe how it ensures predictable and reliable delivery of results.

If both input and output schemas exist, analyze how they together establish a clear data contract in the workflow `{name}`, ensuring smooth data flow and compliance with the Serverless Workflow Specification.
input_output_schema.condition: {{ 'schemas' in element and element.schemas and element.schemas | length > 0 }}

questions: Display the full code of the input schema (if it exists) for the workflow `{name}` and provide a detailed explanation of its structure and purpose.
If the output schema exists, display its full code for the workflow `{name}` and explain how it defines the format and structure of the resulting data.
Show the complete code for both the input and output schemas (if they exist) in the workflow `{name}`, and describe how they work together to validate and structure data.
Highlight specific sections of the input schema code in `{name}` (if it exists) that enforce data constraints, and explain their significance.
If the output schema code exists in `{name}`, identify and explain sections that ensure compatibility with downstream systems.
showing_and_explaining_schemas.condition: {{ 'schemas' in element and element.schemas and element.schemas | length > 0 }}

questions: Break down the core components of the specs files located in the `specs/` directory and referenced in the workflow `{name}`, detailing their structure, purpose, and how they define the APIs or services that the workflow interacts with.

Explain how the specs files (with paths starting in `specs/`) referenced in the workflow `{name}` define interoperability standards for the specific services used by the workflow, ensuring seamless integration across platforms and tools.

Discuss the rules and conventions outlined in the specs files (found in `specs/`) that are relevant to the specific services used in the workflow `{name}`, including request/response schemas, error handling protocols, and security requirements.

Analyze how the specs files located in the `specs/` directory define critical aspects such as retries, data transformations, and error codes for the specific services used in the workflow `{name}`, and discuss their relevance to ensuring robust service interactions.

Describe the role of the specs files (found under `specs/`) in ensuring that the services used by the workflow `{name}` adhere to defined standards, supporting scalability, portability, and maintainability of those services within the workflow.
specs_understanding.condition: {{ 'specs' in element and element.specs and element.specs | length > 0 }}

questions: Explain how the workflow `{name}` utilizes the specs files located in the `specs/` directory to define interactions with APIs or services, highlighting the specific services described in those files.

Identify specific parts of the specs files within the `specs/` directory that are critical to the workflow `{name}`, and describe how these parts guide the workflow’s integration with the relevant services.

Analyze how the specs files located in the `specs/` directory influence the workflow `{name}` by defining rules for error handling, retries, and request/response formats, ensuring consistent interactions with the services described.

Describe the interaction between the workflow `{name}` and the specs files located in the `specs/` directory, focusing on how the workflow dynamically applies the rules or constraints defined in the specs files to achieve its objectives for specific services.
workflow_and_specs_alignment.condition: {{ 'specs' in element and element.specs and element.specs | length > 0 }}

questions: Identify any missing components in the workflow `{name}` that could impact its execution or reliability, such as states or transitions.
Analyze the structure of the workflow `{name}` and highlight areas where modularity or reusability could be improved.
Discuss whether the workflow `{name}` adequately handles error scenarios, and suggest improvements for error handling or retries.
Examine the transitions between states in the workflow `{name}` and identify any inconsistencies or potential bottlenecks.
Evaluate whether the workflow `{name}` supports sufficient scalability and suggest changes to enhance its scalability.

Assess the properties defined in the properties file of the workflow `{name}` to identify any missing or incomplete entries that might limit its functionality, and explain how these properties should be accessed in the workflow using `$SECRET.[PROPERTY_KEY]`.

Evaluate how the annotations and descriptions in the workflow `{name}` align with the properties in the properties file, and suggest improvements to ensure clarity and alignment with the specification. Additionally, describe how these properties are or should be referenced in the workflow using `$SECRET.[PROPERTY_KEY]`.

Discuss whether the properties in the properties file for workflow `{name}` adequately define execution constraints, such as timeouts or retries, and suggest enhancements if needed. Explain how these constraints can be implemented in the workflow using `$SECRET.[PROPERTY_KEY]`.

Identify any gaps in the configuration of the properties file for workflow `{name}` that could hinder its deployment or portability across environments. Provide examples of how missing properties should be referenced in the workflow using `$SECRET.[PROPERTY_KEY]`.

Analyze the properties file for workflow `{name}` and determine if additional entries are needed to better document or manage the workflow. Discuss how these new properties can be utilized in the workflow using `$SECRET.[PROPERTY_KEY]`.
property_and_metadata_gaps.condition: {{ 'properties' in element and element.properties and element.properties != '' }}

questions: Examine the input schema in the workflow `{name}` (if it exists) to identify any missing constraints or data validations.
Evaluate the output schema in the workflow `{name}` (if it exists) and discuss whether it sufficiently defines the structure of the produced data.
Discuss whether the input and output schemas in `{name}` establish a clear data contract and suggest improvements if gaps exist.
Identify any schema mismatches or ambiguities in `{name}` that could lead to data handling issues during execution.
Analyze the use of schemas in `{name}` and determine whether additional fields or constraints are needed to ensure data integrity.
schema_and_data_gaps.condition: {{ 'schemas' in element and element.schemas and element.schemas | length > 0 }}

questions: Identify areas where the workflow `{name}` does not fully align with the Serverless Workflow Specification, particularly in its use of the specs files located in the `specs/` directory, and suggest corrections.

Discuss whether the workflow `{name}` adheres to all rules defined in the specification and the referenced specs files in the `specs/` directory, highlighting any gaps in compliance for the services it integrates with.

Evaluate the workflow `{name}` to determine if additional features or constraints from the specification or specs files in the `specs/` directory could enhance its functionality, particularly for its service interactions.

Analyze the implementation of the workflow `{name}` and identify areas where adherence to the specification and proper utilization of specs files in the `specs/` directory could be improved, ensuring better integration with defined services.

Provide recommendations to close any gaps in `{name}` that may hinder its interoperability or compliance with the specification and the specs files in the `specs/` directory, particularly for its service-oriented operations.
specs_alignment_gaps.condition: {{ 'specs' in element and element.specs and element.specs | length > 0 }}
